responses.summary: Privacy/type leak closed. Added `def num: if type == "number" then . else 0 end;` to the jq ingest filter at tests/e2e/token-analytics.sh:148-149. All 4 token fields now route through `| num` so strings/objects/arrays coerce to 0. Timestamp similarly type-guarded — non-string timestamps coerce to empty. Numeric outputs floored to integers. New test `test_privacy_string_values_coerced_to_zero` (tests/test-token-spike.sh) ingests a malicious transcript with `"USER_SECRET_INPUT"`, `"ASSISTANT_SECRET_OUTPUT"`, `{"nested":"TOOL_ARGS_SECRET"}`, and `["array","payload"]` in usage fields and asserts: (a) none of those strings appear anywhere in history.jsonl, (b) totals reflect ONLY the second well-formed message (in=42, out=7), (c) malicious fields all coerce to 0.
responses.verify_at: tests/e2e/token-analytics.sh:147-156 (`def num` + .|num applied to all 4 fields + timestamp type-guard); tests/test-token-spike.sh test_privacy_string_values_coerced_to_zero

responses.summary: Flat-baseline false-positive suppressed. Added minimum-spread floor at tests/e2e/token-analytics.sh:308-321: threshold = center + max(SIGMA*spread, 5% of center, 1000 abs tokens). Without the floor, MAD=0 → threshold=center → any 1-token uptick fires. Two new tests (test_flat_baseline_small_uptick_does_not_fire and test_flat_baseline_large_jump_still_fires) cover both directions: 5 baseline at 1000, candidate 1100 → no spike (suppressed by 1000 abs floor); 5 baseline at 1000, candidate 50000 → spike fires (floor doesn't mask real regressions). The 5% / 1000 numbers are heuristic and documented inline.
responses.verify_at: tests/e2e/token-analytics.sh:308-321 (threshold awk block with margin floor); tests/test-token-spike.sh:test_flat_baseline_*

responses.summary: test_median_not_mean now asserts the contrast directly. Test runs the analytics twice on the same fixture: once with `--metric median` (must warn) and once with `--metric mean` (must NOT warn — outlier inflates mean+stdev). Both branches now exercised; if anyone swaps the branches in the if/else at token-analytics.sh:299-313, the test fails.

Concurrent-ingest TOCTOU race closed via mkdir-based atomic lock. Wrapped the seen-file/append window with `mkdir "$HISTORY_FILE.lock.d"` (atomic on POSIX), with a 50-attempt × 0.1s wait and 5-minute stale-lock takeover. Lock removed via RETURN trap. New test test_concurrent_ingest_no_duplicate_session runs two ingests in parallel and asserts the result is exactly 1 record / 1 unique session_id (without the lock, this race produces 2 records).
